comics.title: Home Remedies
comics.alt: As always, you are permitted to call one person for guidance, but that person must be a grandparent.

comics.title: Bazookasaurus
comics.alt: In contrast to the deep booming sound associated with the cannon in pop culture depictions, recent studies show it actually made more of a 'toot toot!' noise.

comics.title: Solar Warning
comics.alt: This replaces the previous solar activity watch, which was issued last month when the sun took off its sunglasses.

comics.title: Electric Vehicles
comics.alt: Now that I've finally gotten an electric vehicle, I'm never going back to an acoustic one.

comics.title: Dental Formulas
comics.alt: I mean, half of these are undefined. And your multiplication dots are too low; they look like decimal points.

comics.title: Little Red Dots
comics.alt: After a lot of analysis, I've determined that they're actually big red dots; they're just very far away.

comics.title: Amperage
comics.alt: Oh, and do you have any tips on how to vacuum up copper that's melted into your carpet?

comics.title: Eliminating the Impossible
comics.alt: 'If you've eliminated a few possibilities and you can't think of any others, your weird theory is proven right' isn't quite as rhetorically compelling.

comics.title: Plums
comics.alt: My icebox plum trap easily captured William Carlos Williams. It took much less work than the infinite looping network of diverging paths I had to build in that yellow wood to ensnare Robert Frost.

comics.title: SNEWS
comics.alt: People say setting of fireworks indoors is dangerous, but I looked at their energy release and it's like 10^-40 foe; totally negligible.

comics.title: Bad Map Projection: Zero Declination
comics.alt: 'The zero line in WMM2025 passes through a lot of population centers; I wonder what year the largest share of the population lived in a zone of less than 5° of declination,' he thought, derailing all other tasks for the rest of the day.

comics.title: Installation
comics.alt: Do YOU remember the skylight being this big?

comics.title: Carbon Dating
comics.alt: This dating is corroborated by the presence of stone tools at the site, rather than earlier and less effective helium ones.

comics.title: Dinosaurs And Non-Dinosaurs
comics.alt: Staplers are actually in Pseudosuchia, making them more closely related to crocodiles than to dinosaurs.

comics.title: Binary Star
comics.alt: The discovery of a fully typographical star system comes with a big asterisk.

comics.title: Groundhog Day Meaning
comics.alt: Originally, the ceremony used a variety of rodents and mustelids, but over time most people agreed it made sense to standardize on a specific individual ground squirrel in Pennsylvania.

comics.title: Proof Without Content
comics.alt: There's also a proof without content of a conjecture without content, but it's left as an exercise for the reader.

comics.title: Chemical Formula
comics.alt: Some of the atoms in the molecule are very weakly bound.

comics.title: Early Arthropods
comics.alt: 'Ugh, I'm never going to be like spiders. My descendants will all just be normal arthropods who mind their own busines and don't do anything weird.' --The ancestor of a bunch of eusocial insects

comics.title: Double-Pronged Extension Cord
comics.alt: 'Oh, and can I borrow 50 sacks of loose flour, a pile of lithium-ion batteries, a bucket of bleach, and a bucket of vinega--' 'NO!!!!!!'
